evidence-binding: n1-relax-scheduler ae07e8754897

# n1-relax-scheduler — R1 + R2a + R2b, RED-first, one slice at a time

## RED (pre-fix failing signatures, captured BEFORE each slice's implementation)
RED: R1 (test-adaptive-node.js, pre-impl) — `#641-R1-CORUN t2: R1 co-opens the write w behind the live read (NOT write_awaits_drain), got {"opened":[],"reason":"write_awaits_drain"}`; `#641-R1-A: hold is labeled serialDegradeReason:parallel_writes_off, got undefined` (+ R1-B/C/D/E `serialDegradeReason … got undefined`). R2a (pre-impl) — validation-allowband: `pv.isScratchObservableSurface is not a function` (throw); handoff: `#641-R2a-refuse[code-reviewer]: … refuses plan_invalid, got {"result":"in-grammar"}` (+ security-reviewer/main-session-gate, + `observes_scope_unsupported … got undefined`). R2b (pre-impl, test-adaptive-node.js) — `#641-R2b-A: observes:scratch gate ∥ docs/decisions writer CO-OPENS legless over a dirty parent, got {"opened":[],"reason":"write_awaits_drain","degrade":"parent_dirty"}`; `#641-R2b-CARD: the observation contract is pinned …, got undefined`. (B/C/D/E holds already green — those are the byte-identical parent_dirty holds R2b leaves untouched.)

## GREEN (post-minimal-implementation, all suites)
GREEN: `adaptive-node tests passed (1551 assertions)` (R1 co-run + merge-fence + crash-reconcile + fail-closed matrix A–E + AC5 byte-identity + #588-MIXED updated to co-open + #588-MIXED-LEGLESS-PIN + R2b A–E + R2b-CARD); `validation-allowband guard passed (32 assertions; 5 validator-referenced prose files all kept as CODE)` (R2a band predicate); `adaptive-handoff tests passed (135 assertions)` (R2a observes:scratch freeze accept on adversarial-verifier, typed plan_invalid refuse on code-reviewer/security-reviewer/main-session-gate + observes_scope_unsupported + hash-coverage + handoff end-to-end); `Workflow walkthrough simulation passed`.

## Edition regen + sync parity (GREEN)
GREEN: `npm run sync:editions` → `edition-sync: write complete (6 file(s) updated)` (regenerated the codex twins + gitlab/gitea forge-renamed ports of plan-validator + adaptive-node from canonical). `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` → `10 forge aggregator ports, 24 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, and 27 byte-identical groups in parity with canonical` (exit 0). `node scripts/validate-script-sync.js` → `OK: 24 common scripts, 27 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families … in sync` (exit 0; the adaptive-schema byte-identical group is untouched → still in sync).

## Four-chain (#307 cross-edition obligation) — GREEN
GREEN: `KAOLA_RUN_CHAINS_CONCURRENCY=serial npm test` → `FOURCHAIN_EXIT:0`. All four ran (claude && codex && gitlab && gitea): `Workflow walkthrough simulation passed` (claude), `Kaola-Workflow walkthrough simulation passed` (codex), `GitLab workflow walkthrough` + `GitLab Codex workflow` (gitlab), `Gitea workflow walkthrough` + `Gitea Codex workflow` (gitea); each chain ends `generate-routing-surfaces --check: all 12 surfaces byte-match the skeleton`. Serial mode used to avoid the known concurrent-under-load flake (a load characteristic, not a regression). The GitHub-API/`TLS handshake timeout`/rate-limit lines in the log are claim-hardening network probes (handled gracefully — that suite passed 169 assertions); `PASS (b2) summary line for c2 is FAIL` is a test-run-chains self-test fixture (a PASS, asserting a FAIL summary is surfaced).

## Slice summary
- R1 (default-on, structural): relaxed ONLY the G2 `write_awaits_drain` else-branch in runOpenReady. On a write-only frontier behind live reads it now attempts a LEG-CONTAINED lane-group open (a lone writer = size-1 group, reusing selectSpeculativeWriteGroup #596 verbatim) under EXACTLY four fail-closed preconditions, each labeled with a typed `serialDegradeReason` on the byte-identical hold: (1) `!legCoupled` → `parallel_writes_off` (G12 kill-switch); (2) `liveGroupId` → `lane_group_live` (G8); (3) `parentCarriesProductionDirt` → `parent_dirty` (G5); (4) selectSpeculativeWriteGroup empty → `overlaps_live_writer` | `parallel_safe_indeterminate` (G6/G7/G13). G1 read-first partition UNCHANGED; G4 `merge_awaits_read_drain` merge fence retained (proven by the merge-fence test); the #588 pin STAYS for LEGLESS writers via precondition (1).
- R2a (validator freeze contract): added the hash-covered `observes` ## Nodes column to parseNodes; freeze-refuses `observes: scratch` on any non-adversarial-verifier role (`observes_scope_role_invalid`), an unknown scope (`observes_scope_unsupported`), or a write set (`observes_scope_not_read`). Exposed the co-open legality predicate adaptive-node consumes: `isScratchObservableSurface` (allowband docs MINUS #547 test-consumed prose) + `scratchObservableWriteSet` (writer set ⊆ that ∪ its own .cache evidence). Hash-coverage is automatic (the column lives inside the hashed ## Nodes region).
- R2b (adaptive-node consumer): over a DIRTY parent (R1's leg path unsound), a single LEGLESS writer co-opens behind live reads IFF every live read is an `observes: scratch` adversarial-verifier AND the writer's declared set satisfies scratchObservableWriteSet; else byte-identical `parent_dirty` hold. buildDispatch PINS the observation contract (`verdict from .cache evidence of closed nodes + scratch only; do not read the worktree tree or diff`) on an opened observes:scratch gate's card (conditional field ⇒ byte-identical for every other node).

## AC5 — serial-fallback byte-identity
GREEN: `#641-AC5` asserts KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0 + no running set → exactly one serial write, NO lane_group, and NO `serialDegradeReason` field (byte-identical). The relaxed else-branch is only reachable WITH a running set of live reads, which the no-running-set serial fallback never enters.

## Changed-file list (11 of 16 declared; touched nothing else)
- scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js (+ 3 ports: plugins/kaola-workflow/…, plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-…, plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-…) — canonical edited, ports regenerated by sync:editions.
- scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js (+ 3 ports: codex twin + gitlab/gitea forge-renamed) — canonical edited, ports regenerated.
- scripts/test-adaptive-node.js, scripts/test-validation-allowband.js, scripts/test-adaptive-handoff.js.

## Defensively-declared files UNDER-WRITTEN (skip-reason)
- scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js (+ its 3 byte-identical copies): SKIPPED — no forge-neutral constant needed. The `observes`/`scratch` grammar is fully expressible inside plan-validator (freeze) + adaptive-node (consumer), both of which regenerate their ports via sync:editions; the annotation token stays a literal in those two aggregators. The adaptive-schema byte-identical group is confirmed still in sync (validate-script-sync green).
- scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js: SKIPPED — the scheduler change forced NO integration-assertion update; the walkthrough passes unchanged (its else-branch scenarios don't depend on the write_awaits_drain hold vs co-open shape). Confirmed green.
